title: Art, Drama, and Music Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach courses in drama, music, and the arts including fine and applied art, such as painting and sculpture, or design and crafts. Includes both teachers primarily engaged in teaching and those who do a combination of teaching and research.

tasks.text: Explain and demonstrate artistic techniques.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Evaluate and grade students' class work, performances, projects, assignments, and papers.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prepare students for performances, exams, or assessments.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Initiate, facilitate, and moderate classroom discussions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare and deliver lectures to undergraduate or graduate students on topics such as acting techniques, fundamentals of music, and art history.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare course materials, such as syllabi, homework assignments, and handouts.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Maintain student attendance records, grades, and other required records.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Keep abreast of developments in the field by reading current literature, talking with colleagues, and participating in professional conferences.
